notes: Policy-based tiered storage management; became universal industry practice absorbed into storage platform features.
Pools of storage at different cost/performance tiers managed by ILM policy; now the universal storage architecture.
Consumer tracking technology cited for retail automation and supply chain; adoption was slower than implied but warehousing RFID became standard.
Wireless LAN cited as key ingredient for laptop productivity but security/cost concerns delaying enterprise adoption.
Partner management and value chain integration technology; cited as enabling extended enterprise management.
CRM systems cited as key IT spend driver from consumer empowerment and loyalty program trends.
Real-time click-stream and consumer behavior analytics cited as transformative; correctly predicted the adtech and big data analytics industry.
Not named directly but the per-processor licensing crisis Kastner identified directly foreshadowed the SaaS subscription model shift.
IT systems required for SOX financial controls and reporting; compliance costs cited as several hundred thousand to several million dollars annually.
